result:
I’d make every human mind come pre-loaded with a single, un-erasable piece of code: the certain knowledge that every other mind is as entire and irreplicable as one’s own.  
Not pity, not politeness—just the bone-level recognition that “I am somebody” and “you are somebody” are the same sentence spoken from opposite ends of the room. From that one firmware upgrade, every cruelty that depends on forgetting the fullness of other people would short-circuit: war, slavery, casual indifference, the slow violence of inherited poverty, the fast violence of bombs. We’d still disagree, still compete, still get angry, but we’d do it while being structurally unable to treat anyone as background noise. The world wouldn’t become perfect, only finally negotiable.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
